explanation: Active encryption in progress. Exfil-first ransomware pattern confirmed: large outbound transfers (multi-MB) to suspicious external IPs (91.215.85.47, 185.142.98.12, 45.153.241.81) in prior windows, now followed by mass .locked extension changes with high-entropy file contents. Multiple files across Documents, Desktop, Pictures, ProgramData directories being encrypted simultaneously. svchost.exe process present across all windows likely serving as C2 conduit. Immediate blocking required to halt ongoing encryption.

Benign activity. TiWorker.exe is the legitimate Windows Update worker process. Prior windows show msedge.exe browser activity with inbound traffic from CDN IP 151.101.1.69 (Fastly), temp file creation consistent with browser caching, and a backup zip. No extension changes, no outbound exfiltration, no suspicious process trees. Temp file has normal entropy. No Defender disable events found.

Benign activity. Sparse telemetry showing standard office workflow: EXCEL.EXE document editing, cleanmgr.exe disk cleanup utility, msedge.exe browser session. Document edits show normal small size_delta changes. Temp file creation consistent with Office autosave. No extension changes, no suspicious network activity, no process anomalies. All processes have legitimate parent chains.

Benign browser download and backup activity. Three browser processes (firefox, chrome, msedge) across windows performing large file downloads from legitimate CDN IPs: 151.101.1.69 (Fastly) and 104.16.132.229 (Cloudflare). All network traffic is inbound (downloads), not outbound (exfil). Large backup_011.zip (44MB) consistent with backup_operations pattern. cleanmgr.exe is legitimate Windows disk cleanup. No file extension changes, no suspicious processes, no Defender tampering, no outbound data exfiltration. Zero-delta file reads are normal indexer/AV scan behavior.
